candidate.reason: Owner-sharded allgather does not materialize into e2e throughput because the current dense selected-hot-weight ABI forces padding plus pack/unpack layout conversion, and about half of calls fall back.

timing_profiles.note: For MORI SDMA stages, use wall_issue_ms rather than CUDA event gpu_ms because the timing hook records default-stream events while MORI transport uses its own behavior.

owner_gather_call_mix.interpretation: No successful owner-gather call had pad factor 1.0. Successful calls still sent a padded owner-shard payload with two rows per owner rank, so the candidate did not get the low-payload case from the raw primitive microbench.

root_cause: The retained MORI SDMA path copies owner rows directly into the final dense selected-hot-weight tensor and reduces that tensor in-place.

The torch_allgather candidate must first build an owner-sharded padded tensor, allgather it, then unpack rows back into selected order because downstream grouped MLP expects the dense selected-hot-weight ABI.
The actual top-8 hot expert plans were not one-hot-per-owner. Successful owner-gather calls had pad factor 2.0, and pad factors 3.0 or 4.0 fell back to allreduce.
Thus the candidate sent equal-or-more effective rows and added pack/unpack bookkeeping; the raw primitive advantage was erased.
next_direction: Do not promote torch_allgather as a drop-in forward hot-weight backend.
Keep the standard-EP hot-helper layout as the truth.
Make hot/cold/helper rows and owner-sharded hot-weight buffers first-class in the dispatcher layout so the helper grouped MLP consumes owner-sharded hot weights directly.
Avoid reconstructing dense selected-order hot_weight unless a downstream consumer really requires it.
If owner-sharded movement is retried, gate it on pad factor 1.0 or a direct owner-shard consumer.
